user_story.title: Manage pets through their lifecycle
user_story.text: As a store operator, I can create, update, find, and delete pets so the catalog reflects available inventory.

acceptance_criteria.title: Pet lifecycle acceptance criteria
acceptance_criteria.text: Given a valid pet payload, when the operator creates or updates a pet, then the API persists the change and returns the current pet representation. Given an obsolete pet, when the operator deletes it, then the pet is no longer returned in inventory searches.

incidents.title: Status filter mismatch after pet updates
incidents.text: A pet updated from available to sold was still visible in the available inventory filter for several minutes. The incident points to missing consistency checks between updatePet and search behavior.

frontmatter.resolution: added index refresh and regression monitoring

openapi_operations.summary: Add a new pet to the store
openapi_operations.description: Creates a pet resource from a Pet payload.
Updates an existing pet resource using a full Pet payload.

diagrams.title: Pet lifecycle sequence
diagrams.source:
@startuml
actor Operator
participant Widget
participant PetAPI
Operator -> Widget: Save pet changes
Widget -> PetAPI: addPet / updatePet
PetAPI --> Widget: Pet representation
Operator -> Widget: Remove obsolete pet
Widget -> PetAPI: deletePet
@enduml

findings.title: Incident references status search not covered by feature docs
findings.description: The incident mentions findPetsByStatus, but the feature story and acceptance criteria only cover addPet, updatePet, and deletePet.
findings.recommended_action: Add explicit status-search consistency coverage or link the incident to a separate inventory-search feature.

findings.title: Delete success response is not described
findings.description: Acceptance criteria says deleted pets disappear from search, but does not document the expected delete response behavior.
findings.recommended_action: Clarify expected delete response and follow-up verification behavior.

findings.title: Diagram combines create and update operations
findings.description: The diagram uses one interaction for addPet and updatePet, which can hide divergent validation paths.
findings.recommended_action: Split create and update into separate steps if the review needs path-level traceability.

generated_test_ideas.title: Updated status is reflected in status search
generated_test_ideas.given_when_then: Given an available pet, when the operator updates its status to sold, then available search no longer returns the pet and sold search does.
generated_test_ideas.rationale: Covers the linked incident and validates cross-operation consistency.

generated_test_ideas.title: Deleted pet is absent from inventory lookup
generated_test_ideas.given_when_then: Given an existing pet, when the operator deletes it, then subsequent inventory lookup does not return it.
generated_test_ideas.rationale: Covers the acceptance criteria around obsolete pet removal.

recommended_next_actions: Decide whether status-search consistency belongs to this feature or a separate inventory-search feature.
Document delete response expectations in acceptance criteria.
Split create/update diagram steps if reviewers need one operation per step.
audit.model_context_hint: Review status-search consistency before approving the pet lifecycle docs.
